name: CONGENITAL LOBAR OVERINFLATION
definition: CONGENITAL LOBAR EMPHYSEMA (technically inaccurate term) progressive overdistension of one / multiple lobes

demographics.Location: LUL (42-43%) > RML (32-35%) > RUL (20%) > either lower lobe (< 1%)

dominant_finding: hazy masslike dense opacity immediately following birth ← delayed clearance of lung fluid in
distribution: LUL (42-43%) > RML (32-35%) > RUL (20%) > either lower lobe (< 1%)
discriminating_features: hazy masslike dense opacity immediately following birth ← delayed clearance of lung fluid in
hyperlucent overinflated lobe (after clearing of fluid via lymphatic + capillary system)
compression atelectasis of adjacent ipsilateral lobes

general: hazy masslike dense opacity immediately following birth ← delayed clearance of lung fluid in
hyperlucent overinflated lobe (after clearing of fluid via lymphatic + capillary system)
compression atelectasis of adjacent ipsilateral lobes
widely separated but organized vascular markings
mass of homogeneously high SI on T2WI
